text:
Excited to share that I joined ESPN Central Texas this spring as a TikTok Creator Intern 🎙️

Building out their social platforms from scratch — creating content several times a week, attending live broadcasts, studio podcasts, and in-field sports productions.

Combining sports media with content creation is exactly where I want to be, and getting to do it for a real broadcast operation as a sophomore feels like a real head start.

More content dropping soon. Follow ESPN Central Texas on TikTok 👇

50 million views on TikTok. That number still doesn't fully compute.

@NotJax started as a way to document what I was working on — and it turned into $15K+ in brand deals, partnerships with Gymshark, Snow, Locket, Ailith AI, and Recapture Livestream, and a 3-month livestream campaign that rewired how I think about monetization.

18.7K followers. 22M views in the last year alone.

Biggest lesson I keep coming back to: consistency compounds. Show up every day, iterate constantly, and the data starts working for you instead of against you.

Next chapter is getting bigger 🔒

Just finished my first semester as VP of Recruitment for Baylor's S3E Club — and the numbers were meaningful.

→ Reached out to 65 Texas DECA programs
→ Contacted 180+ MBA and BBA students
→ Ran multiple promotional events and class visits

The S3E (Sports, Strategy, Sales & Entertainment) program consistently places students with the Cowboys, Mavericks, Spurs, Golden Knights, Avalanche, and Curb Records — every single year.

If you're a Baylor student who wants to work in sports or entertainment business, come find us. The connections this program opens up are real.

150K views in 30 days from a brand new account. That was the Insight Timer UGC campaign.

30 videos. Performance-based pay. A tight feedback loop where every upload taught me something about what hooks actually convert vs. what just looks good on paper.

UGC has honestly taught me more about consumer psychology and conversion analytics than any class I've taken. The feedback is immediate, the data is real, and there's no hiding from a bad hook.

If you're trying to break into brand partnerships — start creating for brands before they pay you to. The reel is the application.

Wrapped up the Peer Leader Program this semester — one of the most underrated things I've done at Baylor.

Led weekly sessions for 26 freshmen, helped them navigate their first Foster School semester, and organized individual check-ins throughout.

The program's students had an 8.3% higher satisfaction and retention rate vs. non-participants. That number is what makes it feel worth it.

Teaching forces you to actually understand what you're explaining. Every week I walked out of those sessions having learned something too.

Quick win worth sharing: just crossed 6,000 followers on Instagram with 44.5M lifetime reel views.

This account started as a personal page. No strategy, no system. At some point I started treating it like a real content operation — scheduling, analytics, A/B testing formats, studying what held attention.

15.7M accounts reached in the last 30 days alone.

The platform rewards people who study it. That's true for Instagram, TikTok, and honestly most things worth doing.
